input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen when targeting .NET 10. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work anymore in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In .NET 10 (and actually starting with .NET 9 in some previews), the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ and MAUI moved toward edge-to-edge rendering. The recommended approach changed.


For .NET 10, the better solution is using `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior, which automatically adjusts the view when the keyboard appears, since the older `adjustResize` approach is no longer supported on modern Android APIs. The real solution for .NET 10 is to set `SoftInputMode` to `AdjustResize` in the manifest while also using `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` to respond to keyboard insets and adjust padding accordingly. Or there's a simpler MAUI-specific approach I'm considering.
